meta.granularity: directory/module; file-level only where a file merits individual comment
meta.desc_style: retrieval-oriented: keyword-bearing (key types, subsystem vocabulary, R-numbers), <=140 chars
meta.checker: cargo xtask filemap - both directions, failures not warnings; runs in CI's lint job

entry.desc: agent orientation: file-map-first discovery, make-it-so gating, never-commit, Notorolla read-only, pointers to getstarted/standard
public front page: one-paragraph pitch, links to the PoC plan and the live planning dashboard, dual-license notice
workspace manifest: member list src/rust/*, workspace.package, workspace.dependencies (checkpoint-gated), dev-profile opt-level overrides
toolchain pin 1.95.0, rustfmt+clippy components, cross targets: linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, wasm32
cargo-deny license allowlist, GPL-free enforcement; new licenses enter here with their dependency checkpoint
ci.yml jobs: lint (fmt, clippy -D warnings), test (windows), cross (linux/mac/wasm checks), license (cargo-deny)
config.toml: cargo alias xtask = run --package rev-xtask
bundled non-crate assets + asset.json manifest (source, version, sha256, licence, author) feeding R-1514 attribution and R-1515 credits
developer/agent utilities, not shipped, not a crate: agent_readme.md indexes them; dump_log.py reads the observation.revlog log. stdlib-only
Source Sans 3 (ui role: regular/italic/bold/bold-italic) + JetBrains Mono NL (numeric role: regular/bold), OFL-1.1, with licence texts
revision.md, requirements (R-numbers), poc plan, getstarted, coding standard, HIG inventory + decisions register, plan/schema/filemap docs
archived proposals of completed plan items: what was decided, why, and deviations-from-proposal. History, not current guidance
rev-core: model - event, phrase, phrase_instance, track, tick 5040ppq, note_number, tuning materialize, scale mask, Command enum; WASM-able
rev-engine: the RT audio engine. Engine::process, the four-channel seam, cpal and offline drivers, allocation guard (eng-01)
rev-store: SQLite - schema/DDL, exec (commands+inverses), journal, undo/redo, replay, genesis, query catalog (all SQL), v_realized
rev-midi: midir - enumeration (R-601), driver timestamps + clock correlation (R-603), thru fast path (R-605), live/capture fork
rev-ui-mech: winit/softbuffer/tiny-skia/cosmic-text - WindowId-keyed windows, CPU surface, frame loop, input, R-907; only crate with Win32
rev-ui-kit: widgets in the Notorolla-exhibit skin (R-712) - button, pop-up, toggle, numeric display, shuttle, locators; mechanism client
rev-app: composition root binary - wiring, command dispatch, view state; no musical logic in any frontend
rev-testkit: test support (dev-dep only) - fixtures, golden-master comparators (spectra dB, meters, bit-identity), proptest strategies
rev-xtask: repo automation. schema, filemap, and plan checkers, plus tmon (the kill-9 store durability gate, rec-02); perf pending
golden masters: ui/ PNG screenshots compared bit-identically; .f32 audio + JSON sidecars (sample_rate, seed, provenance) from dsp-02
rev-log: the observation log. Log handle, four levels, dotted creators, bounded channel to one writer thread; rusqlite only (eng-01 §9)
observation log DDL: session + entry (creator, level, text, detail JSON, keep). One forever file, WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, pruned by size
log writer thread: batched inserts, size-capped pruning by used pages (page_count - freelist), stderr echo, drop-and-count on a full channel
OS application-data directory per platform, dependency-free; default log path observation.revlog
the seam: rtrb rings + PositionCell. EngineSession (app) and RtPort (RT); commands refuse, observations drop-and-count, returns never drop
Position + PositionCell: the seqlock the counter reads. Latest-wins, wait-free writer; carries clock correlation and latency terms
Command/What: every command time-stamped, frequencies never note numbers (R-312). ChunkHandle + Garbage; the RT thread never frees
engine observations: POD code + 3 args on the RT side, rendered to prose app-side. Creator, Level, Code catalogue
allocation guard: global allocator wrapper + per-thread RT flag, debug/test only; exempt while panicking so unwinding does not double-panic
cpal driver: device selection with REVISION_AUDIO_DEVICE override, OpenReport logged at open, stream opens once and never stops
offline driver: same core, no device. The render-twice bit-identity path (R-1402), how CI runs the RT discipline, f32 WAV writer
app side of the seam: holds EngineSession + Device, pumps observations into rev-log and frees returned garbage each frame
rev-tone: headless first sound. --list, --device, --hz, --seconds, --render FILE.wav (renders twice and compares)
rev-sched: the schedule compiler. Ticks and note numbers in, samples and frequencies out - R-312's boundary made literal (eng-06)
TempoMap: integer-exact segment-anchored tick<->sample in i128, round-half-to-even. Monotonic, deterministic, non-accumulating
Compiler: v_realized windows to Chunk. Notes carry duration (R-402a); onsets gated, durations never truncated (R-405a)
TuneCache: note number to frequency through materialized tunings, cached by id. The one place this conversion happens
node graph as data: closed NodeKind enum, GraphSpec described then compiled to dependency order + buffer assignment; QUANTUM=128
Voice + VoicePool: pre-built pool, start offset for sample-accurate onsets, reclaimed when RELEASE completes, stealing prefers releasing
baked wavetables and the per-instrument registry; linear interpolation, justified by the near-unity playback rate
Param: default value plus fixed a-rate/k-rate. The four-method ramp vocabulary is eng-04's
AudioParam automation: setValue, linear/exponential ramp, setTarget per W3C formulas. Fixed capacity, allocation-free, time in frames
the Padlington voice as a GraphSpec: two seeded read heads, trims, panners, amp gain, filter; Patch play-time params and envelopes
rev-mhall: the first musical sound. Builds MHALL into a throwaway project, compiles and plays it; --tuning, --bpm, --render
rev-rec: capture path end to end (rec-01). Arm a track, play a keyboard, journal notes per frame, replay - headless; --overdub, --project
rev-studio: throwaway record-onto-roll harness (rec-02). Transport strip over the live roll, two tracks, record light, live redraw
the Player view core (ui-08): column model (reorder/hide/resize), fake TrackRow/Block fixtures shaped for ui-09, table + overview painters
Player column-model tests: candidate set, hide/reorder/resize, last-column guard, at_x hit-test, fixtures shaped for the painters
rev-player: Player + Control Bar workspace mockup (ui-08). Multi-window; a palette transport plays the focused Playable; per-window state
rev-dsp: the PADsynth bake (dsp-02). Pure data-in/data-out, no workspace dependency; realfft. R-705/R-706/R-713/R-720
BakeSpec: the twelve bake-relevant fields, Source/Vowel enums; Eq+Hash cache key; seed() derives the bake seed
harmonic amplitude profile A_k = source(k) x formant_mask(k*f0); saw/pulse/voice/tilt, three-formant vowel bank
Gaussian smear into a magnitude spectrum; partial placement with stretch; air blend; the band limit applied to centres, skirts and air
bake/bake_set/bake_with_limit: seeded phase, inverse real FFT, RMS normalization; TABLE_LEN, TABLE_RMS, band_limit, half-octave bases
SplitMix64 and a hand-written FNV-1a: the bake seed must not move with the toolchain
aliasing sweep across the keyboard and the pitch assertion; renders through rev-engine as a dev-dependency. R-720, R-1402
spectral helper for tests: Blackman-Harris analyse, fundamental_hz, centroid_near, worst_inharmonic; the eng-07 pitch debt
assembles the Padlington: rev-dsp bake -> rev_engine::TableSet, sixteen half-octave tables, app-side
scrollable pane geometry: reserved gutters, proportional thumb, zoom cluster placement, content/pane/window spaces. ui-07
rev-pane demo: ruled grid with coordinates, absurd extent, both bar policies - the operable check the goldens cannot make
pane behaviour and goldens: drag snap-back and resume, escape ignored, paging, wheel zoom anchoring
the read-only piano roll (ui-06): notes via TuneCache, log2(hz) x beats, degree+grid ladders, a11y notes-as-data. R-941/943/312
roll drawing and its tests: same-as-engine frequencies, retuning moves notes, octave equidistance, thickness, a11y
playhead follow (ui-06 §8): armed/disarmed by horizontal user scroll, page-not-slide, FOLLOW_TRIGGER/LAND constrained pair. R-947
follow state-machine tests: jump-and-land, take-over, re-arm on locate, zoom anchors playhead, end-of-piece clamp
the Recorder (rec-01): arm/mode, on/off pairing to placed EventSpecs via Correlation+TempoMap, per-frame RecordBatch journaling. R-807
recorder tests: placement on the tick grid, on/off pairing and duration, overdub vs replace, replace undo, commits-as-it-goes durability
MHALL as a project, shared by the rev-mhall and rev-roll demo binaries (rev-testkit is dev-only and cannot ship)
rev-roll demo: MHALL drawn and playing, space plays, home locates and re-arms follow - the ui-06 look
clock-domain correlation (midi-01 §4, R-603): rolling least-squares fit of sample position vs monotonic time; slope is observed sample rate
the fit's tests: clean/jittered/drifting clock, stale-pair rejection, bounded history
note→Hz snapshot (midi-01 §5, R-312): 128-entry lock-free resolution table; also midi-04's remap hook
snapshot tests: resolves exactly as the tuning, silence for unresolved/out-of-range
parsed MIDI Message (note on/off, vel-0 = off) and the LiveKey pairing; Captured = model-side event for capture (R-002)
message parse tests: note on/off, velocity-0, channel identity, level curve
the input Fork (midi-01 §3,§7): a message -> thru ring (frequencies, R-312) + event ring (note numbers, R-002); set_snapshot remaps
fork tests: physics+model split, off-pairing, vel-0, unresolved keys, live snapshot remap
opening a real midir port (midi-01 §1): list() and open() wiring Fork::on_message; enumeration/hot-plug deferred to midi-02
integration: a real midir open proving the types; safe with no device
Live/LiveKey (midi-01 §6): physics + opaque handle crossing the thru ring; the input side of R-312
enumeration + hot-plug (midi-02, R-601/602): poll the port list, diff for arrival/removal, find a remembered device by name
hot-plug diff tests: first-poll arrivals, plug/unplug, remembered-device rebind
live MIDI input manager (midi-02): opens a rev-midi port, hands the callback the engine's ThruSender, polls for hot-plug and auto-reconnect
honest live-path latency estimate (midi-03, R-307 v0): a measurable floor from the observed block size, admitting the buffering it can't see
latency estimate tests: two-block floor, bigger buffer = more, device latency added, the honest 'at least' summary
